spec_match.rationale: The page now frames revert-rule as ignoring the current style rule and shows ordered rules falling back to previous declarations, matching CSS Cascade Level 5 section 7.3.6.

interactive.rationale: Rule toggles, a conflict panel, and a live CSS editor drive visible output and computed-style readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and feature-index promise both describe a visual cascade debugger with per-rule revert-rule fallback.

edge_cases.rationale: The page includes unsupported-browser detection, simulated fallback behavior, and a conflict case where the current rule is ignored.

references.rationale: A labelled references section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5146458504429568 and the CSS Cascade spec.

design_system.rationale: The edited styles use shared CSS variables, button background overrides include color, and the page remains a live demo rather than a static code card.

summary: The debugger now explains and demonstrates revert-rule as same-rule rollback, includes feature detection and a labelled ChromeStatus reference, and has no remaining major critique item.
